assets.title: Assets
assets.description: Nodes, infrastructure, networks, and deployable resources.

identities.title: Identities
identities.description: Accounts, entities, roles, and identity-bearing participants.

observables.title: Observables
observables.description: Conditions, metrics, telemetry, and observable properties.

actions-and-events.title: Actions and Events
actions-and-events.description: Events, injects, workflow steps, and executable actions.

tools-and-artifacts.title: Tools and Artifacts
tools-and-artifacts.description: Features, content, software, and deployable artifacts.

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.description: SDL scenarios, compositions, modules, and authoring constructs.
scenarios.extension_scope: SDL-native scenario authoring constructs, compositions, modules, and scenario-bound declarations that are not UCO cyber object definitions.
relation_rules: May bind to adopted cyber-domain families only when the scenario construct denotes a cyber object, observable, action, relationship, identity, or artifact.
Must remain the container and authoring-intent layer rather than redefining adopted cyber-domain families.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not be used as a synonym for assets, identities, relationships, observables, actions-and-events, or tools-and-artifacts.
Artifact bindings must choose the narrower adopted or adapted family when a field denotes that cyber-domain meaning directly.

tasks-runs-studies.title: Tasks, Runs, and Studies
tasks-runs-studies.description: Execution lifecycle, run records, and study organization.
tasks-runs-studies.extension_scope: ACES experiment workflow concepts for task framing, run lifecycle, study grouping, and analysis organization.
relation_rules: May reference scenarios as the authoring input under evaluation and provenance-and-evidence concepts as recorded outputs.
Must describe experiment organization and lifecycle rather than the cyber-domain assets or actions inside a scenario.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not duplicate scenario authoring semantics, runtime execution state, or external cyber-domain object meaning.
Run or study concepts must not be used to classify nodes, accounts, observables, tools, or events.

apparatus-declarations.title: Apparatus Declarations
apparatus-declarations.extension_scope: ACES apparatus identity, capability, compatibility, and declaration surfaces for processors, backends, and participant implementations.
relation_rules: May bind declared capability vocabularies to adopted or adapted cyber-domain families through concept_bindings.
Must keep apparatus identity and compatibility separate from the cyber-domain objects that an apparatus can process or realize.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not redefine assets, identities, observables, actions, relationships, or tools as apparatus concepts.
Capability bindings must use the canonical cyber-domain family when the declaration names supported cyber-domain vocabulary.

realization-and-disclosure.title: Realization and Disclosure
realization-and-disclosure.description: Instantiation, planning, compilation, and realization artifacts.
realization-and-disclosure.extension_scope: ACES realization semantics for turning underspecified authoring inputs into executable, planned, or disclosed artifacts.
relation_rules: May relate scenarios to apparatus-declarations by describing what an apparatus can realize or disclose.
Must distinguish realization support from the cyber-domain objects being realized.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not be used as a label for realized assets, identities, tools, observables, relationships, or actions themselves.
When a declaration names the kind of cyber object realized, it must bind to the corresponding cyber-domain family rather than this native family.

provenance-and-evidence.title: Provenance and Evidence
provenance-and-evidence.description: Run provenance records, evidence expectations, and audit artifacts.
provenance-and-evidence.extension_scope: ACES provenance, evidence expectation, evidence collection, and audit-record semantics for experiments and execution.
relation_rules: May record observations about scenarios, tasks, runs, studies, apparatus, and cyber-domain objects without redefining those objects.
Must separate evidence/provenance records from the observed asset, identity, observable, action, relationship, or artifact.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not classify the subject of evidence as evidence itself.
Evidence bindings must choose the observed cyber-domain family when the field denotes the observed object rather than the evidence record.

time-and-apparatus.title: Time and Apparatus
time-and-apparatus.description: Clocks, timing constraints, and apparatus-level concerns.
time-and-apparatus.extension_scope: ACES timing, clock, scheduling, synchronization, and apparatus-time semantics that govern execution rather than cyber-domain object identity.
relation_rules: May constrain scenarios, tasks, runs, realization, and apparatus behavior through time or clock declarations.
Must keep timing and clock semantics separate from the cyber-domain event or action whose occurrence is being timed.
non_ambiguity_constraints: Must not be used as a synonym for actions-and-events or observables.
Temporal fields that describe an event meaning must bind to actions-and-events or observables; fields that describe ACES clock policy may bind here.
